name: Saver's Check Cashing II
description_short: Savers 99 Store II is a San Diego-based Latino market offering check cashing, money transfers, and grocery items with strong community reviews for customer service.
description_long:
Savers 99 Store II operates as a hybrid discount grocery store and financial services center located at 2901 National Ave in San Diego's National City neighborhood. The business functions primarily as a small Latino market but has integrated financial services including check cashing and international money transfers to serve its community. Founded to serve the local Latino population, the store has built its reputation through consistent operations and community engagement over multiple years based on customer review history. The business maintains extended hours (7 AM–9 PM daily) to accommodate working customers and maintains a physical location that customers describe as clean and safe.

The company offers multiple services beyond traditional check cashing. Primary services include check cashing, international money transfers (sending money to other countries), ATM access, money orders, and bill payment services. These financial services are integrated into their retail grocery operation, which features fresh meat, vegetables, and authentic Latino food products. The store also operates as a discount retailer, lottery retailer, key duplication service, and convenience store. Reviews consistently mention the ability to "send money to other countries" as a key distinguishing feature alongside their grocery offerings.

Savers 99 Store II distinguishes itself through exceptional customer service ratings (4.6 out of 5 stars across 35 reviews) and a demonstrated commitment to maintaining a safe, clean community space. Multiple reviewers specifically praise the friendly staff and note that regular customers "come here almost everyday." The store positions itself as supporting local Latino community needs by combining affordable grocery shopping with accessible financial services in one location. This integrated approach addresses both food security and financial access for underbanked populations in the area.

The primary limitation is the company's single-location format—service is available only at the San Diego address, making it inaccessible to customers outside the immediate area. While check cashing and money transfer services are confirmed, specific fee structures, maximum check amounts, and transfer limits are not disclosed on the available materials. The store's primary identity remains as a grocery/discount market rather than a specialized financial services provider, which may affect service availability or expertise compared to dedicated check cashing establishments.
pros: Extended daily hours (7 AM–9 PM) seven days a week for convenient access
International money transfer services enabling payments to other countries
Consistently high customer satisfaction (4.6/5 stars) with 25 of 35 reviews rating 5 stars
Clean, safe, and well-maintained facility per multiple customer reviews
Integrated grocery shopping eliminates need for separate retail trip
Friendly, welcoming staff noted specifically in multiple customer testimonials
Affordable pricing on both financial services and retail items per reviews
cons: Single location in San Diego limits accessibility for non-local customers
No published fee schedule or pricing information available for check cashing or transfers
Primary business model is grocery retail, not specialized financial services
No online or remote service options mentioned; in-person only
Limited information on check amount limits, transfer speed, or service scope
best_for: San Diego residents needing convenient check cashing combined with grocery shopping
Latino community members sending remittances to family in other countries
Customers seeking a clean, safe financial services location with friendly service
diagnosis: Savers 99 Store II is best suited for San Diego residents who need check cashing and international money transfer services integrated with grocery shopping in a community-focused environment. The main caveat is that it operates as a single-location Latino market rather than a specialized financial services provider, with no disclosed fee information or remote service options.
